agenda_pressure: Prefer exact reachable-region computation over ambient finite-slice packaging.
Favor reusable order-independent sweep invariants over one-off list encodings.
Use ambient-slice and mixed-terminal counterexamples to keep boundary-only follow-ups low unless they unlock exact computation.
current_bottlenecks: Canonical labeling is still packaged abstractly rather than as an explicit arbitrary-order fold.
The theory lacks a stable semantic invariant for intermediate reverse-sweep states.
Strict degree descent has not yet been converted into exact path-length or saturation-round bounds.
desired_summary_changes: A concrete `List.foldr` theorem for any reverse-topological enumeration of the exact reachable finset.
A reusable suffix or partial-sweep invariant identifying each intermediate fold state with the least predecessor-closed subset of the unprocessed region.
An exact distance-to-base criterion on reachable states that yields finite saturation-round convergence as a corollary.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: For every Γ : List Tp and s : String, (¬ ∃ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) q) ↔ ∀ B ∈ Γ, is_atom B.
Verified counterexample to: For every finite list src and atom s, the class of states p such that p.2 = s and every formula B in p.1 is a subformula of some formula C in src is finite.

Verified counterexample to: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
missing_bridges: A bridge from canonical or least-subset descriptions to an explicit `List.foldr` computation on arbitrary reverse-topological lists.
A bridge from predecessor closure to operator-style least-fixed-point and iterative saturation on the exact reachable region.
A bridge from degree descent to exact path distance and convergence time.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize problems that make the exact reachable finset computationally explicit, either by proving arbitrary reverse-topological fold correctness with a reusable partial-sweep invariant or by deriving exact distance and saturation-round bounds on that same region.
next_direction.label: explicit_fold_and_distance_control
next_direction.rationale: The semantic characterization of acceptance is already complete on the exact reachable DAG, while counterexamples have shown that ambient slices and universal-terminal viewpoints are not the right abstraction; the remaining summary-changing work is executable fold semantics and quantitative convergence.

overexplored_patterns: Ambient same-atom or bounded-slice exactness and separation questions outside the exact reachable region.
Universal-terminal or all-sinks summaries already weakened by mixed-terminal evidence.
Presentation-specific restatements of labeling or least-subset results that do not yield an explicit algorithm or quantitative bound.
theory_snapshot: The theory now has a stable exact-reachable-region core: from any residual root, the reachable region is finite, degree-descending, and acceptance is exactly reachability of the distinguished singleton base state. On that exact region, acceptance already has canonical Boolean-label, least predecessor-closed subset, and topological-order characterizations, so the main structural gap is no longer semantic correctness but explicit computation. The remaining frontier is to turn those abstract descriptions into a concrete arbitrary reverse-topological fold and, in parallel, to extract exact saturation-round and path-length control from the same descent structure. Verified failures of ambient same-atom finiteness and verified mixed-terminal behavior continue to rule out ambient overapproximation and universal-sink summaries as high-value directions.
